rationale: Public Health is 5% of papers but punches above its weight in policy impact — Cochrane reviews, WHO data, outbreak modeling. Fourteen subspecialties match the working organization of accredited schools of public health (epidemiology, biostats, env health, behavioral, policy, global health). I split Epidemiology by method-focus (analytic epi vs surveillance & outbreak) because the COVID years exploded outbreak modeling into its own identity. I kept Biostatistics here rather than under Statistics because biostatistical method papers most often land in PH journals and the identity is 'I'm a biostatistician at a school of PH'. I made Health Services Research and Health Policy two cards — they overlap but HSR is methodologically distinct (econ + epi hybrid) while Health Policy is more normative.

subspecialties.name: Epidemiology
subspecialties.tagline: Cohorts, case-control, causal inference for disease.
covers: chronic disease epi, cancer epi, cardiovascular epi, reproductive epi, life-course epi, study design methodology, causal epi
Am J Epi, IJE, Epidemiology
explicitly_excludes: outbreak/infectious disease epi → Infectious Disease Epi & Outbreak

subspecialties.name: Infectious Disease Epi & Outbreak Modeling
covers: outbreak investigation, infectious disease modeling, vaccine epi, transmission dynamics, syndromic surveillance, COVID-19 epi, AMR surveillance

subspecialties.name: Biostatistics
subspecialties.tagline: Methods for clinical trials, survival, mixed models, missing data.
covers: trial design, survival analysis, longitudinal methods, mixed models, missing data, multiple imputation, group-sequential and adaptive designs
Biometrics, Stat Med, BMC Med Res Methodol
explicitly_excludes: pure mathematical statistics → Mathematics or Statistics & Data Science

subspecialties.name: Global Health
subspecialties.tagline: LMIC health, health systems strengthening, equity.
covers: LMIC clinical and public health interventions, health systems strengthening, neglected tropical diseases, maternal and child health globally, global health equity
Lancet Glob Health, BMJ Glob Health, PLOS Glob Public Health

subspecialties.name: Environmental & Occupational Health
subspecialties.tagline: Air, water, chemical exposure, occupational risk.
covers: air pollution health effects, water and sanitation, chemical exposure assessment, occupational health, environmental epidemiology, exposome research
Environ Health Perspect, Occup Environ Med

subspecialties.name: Health Policy & Politics
subspecialties.tagline: Insurance, regulation, health systems, comparative policy.
covers: health insurance and financing, regulatory policy, comparative health systems, drug pricing policy, public health law

subspecialties.name: Health Economics & Health Services Research
subspecialties.tagline: Cost-effectiveness, access, quality of care, delivery.
covers: cost-effectiveness analysis, quality of care research, access and equity, payment reform, care delivery models, comparative effectiveness research
Health Aff, Med Care, JHEcon, Health Econ

subspecialties.name: Behavioral & Social Sciences of Health
covers: health behavior change, health communication, social determinants of health, qualitative health research, community-based participatory research

subspecialties.name: Maternal & Child Health
subspecialties.tagline: Reproductive health, perinatal, child wellbeing, adolescent.
covers: maternal health, reproductive health, perinatal outcomes, child development from a population health angle, adolescent health

subspecialties.name: Public Health Nutrition
subspecialties.tagline: Population nutrition, obesity policy, food security.
covers: population nutrition, dietary patterns and chronic disease, food security, obesity at population level, micronutrient deficiency programs

subspecialties.name: Mental Health & Substance Use
subspecialties.tagline: Population mental health, addiction policy, suicide prevention.
covers: population mental health, addiction epidemiology, opioid epi, suicide prevention research, mental health services research

subspecialties.name: Injury & Violence Prevention
subspecialties.tagline: Trauma epi, road safety, gun violence, intimate partner violence.
covers: injury epidemiology, traffic safety, occupational injury, firearm injury epi, IPV prevention research

subspecialties.name: Implementation Science & Quality Improvement
subspecialties.tagline: Getting evidence into practice, QI methodology.
covers: implementation science frameworks, dissemination research, quality improvement methodology, evidence-to-practice translation
Impl Sci, BMJ Qual Saf

subspecialties.name: One Health & Planetary Health
covers: climate change and health, planetary health, zoonotic disease ecology, antimicrobial resistance from a One Health angle, food-system health
Lancet Planet Health

uncovered_concerns: Considered separating Health Informatics — large but lands either in HSR (decision support, EHR research) or under Computer Science & AI (methods). No level-2 home here.
Considered Bioethics — important but ~0.5% of PH papers and most identify with Humanities (philosophy) or Health Policy. Folded into Health Policy.
Considered separating Vaccines Research — papers split between Medicine (clinical trials), Biology (immunology basic), and Infectious Disease Epi (population data). No card for it.
Considered Surveillance Systems — folded under Infectious Disease Epi & Outbreak.
